title: Phase 41 — ToastContext timer cleanup + NotificationData type safety

stories.title: ToastContext: track and cleanup setTimeout timers
stories.description:
In context/ToastContext.tsx, setTimeout() is called in addToast() but the timer ID is not stored. Two issues:
1. If ToastProvider unmounts (edge case), timers keep running and call setToasts on unmounted component.
2. When user manually dismisses a toast via the ✕ button, the auto-dismiss timer still fires later (wasteful).

Fix:
- Add a timerRef = useRef<Map<number, ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>>(new Map()) in ToastProvider
- In addToast(), store the timer: timerRef.current.set(id, setTimeout(...))
- In removeToast(), clear the timer first: clearTimeout(timerRef.current.get(id)); timerRef.current.delete(id)
- Add a useEffect that returns () => { timerRef.current.forEach(clearTimeout); timerRef.current.clear(); } for cleanup on unmount
acceptance: timerRef tracks all active auto-dismiss timers
removeToast() clears the timer before removing from state
useEffect cleanup clears all timers on unmount
No change to toast UI or animation behavior

stories.title: App.tsx: tighten NotificationData type
acceptance: | { type: string } catch-all removed from NotificationData
handleNotificationTap still compiles without TypeScript errors
The else clause in handleNotificationTap handles unknown types
